title: CutoffFunctional
summary: Packages cutoff-dependent quantities whose asymptotic expansion defines a renormalized value.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Packages cutoff-dependent quantities whose asymptotic expansion defines a renormalized value.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows Ambient/PE/Renormalization/CutoffFunctional.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A cutoff-dependent functional together with its Laurent--log expansion. Its mathematical data are: cutoff Value: Obj maps to Pos Real maps to R; expansion: Obj maps to Laurent Log Expansion R.
correspondence.leanType: structure CutoffFunctional (Obj : Type u) (R : Type v) [Zero R]

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The finite-part value of a cutoff functional. In explicit terms, for cf of type cutoff functional obj r, and obj of type obj, its defining expression is finite Part CF.expansion obj.
correspondence.leanType: def renormalizedValue (CF : CutoffFunctional Obj R) (obj : Obj) : R

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The logarithmic anomaly of a cutoff functional. In explicit terms, for cf of type cutoff functional obj r, and obj of type obj, its defining expression is log Anomaly CF.expansion obj.
correspondence.leanType: def anomalyValue (CF : CutoffFunctional Obj R) (obj : Obj) : R

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For CF of type Cutoff Functional Obj R, and obj of type Obj, CF.renormalized Value obj equals CF.expansion obj.coeff 0.
correspondence.leanType: theorem renormalizedValue_eq_coeff_zero (CF : CutoffFunctional Obj R) (obj : Obj) : CF.renormalizedValue obj = (CF.expansion obj).coeff 0

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For CF of type Cutoff Functional Obj R, and obj of type Obj, CF.anomaly Value obj equals CF.expansion obj.log Coeff.
correspondence.leanType: theorem anomalyValue_eq_logCoeff (CF : CutoffFunctional Obj R) (obj : Obj) : CF.anomalyValue obj = (CF.expansion obj).logCoeff

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Top-level finite-part accessor for a cutoff functional. In explicit terms, for obj a type, r a type, cf of type cutoff functional obj r, and obj of type obj, its defining expression is CF.renormalized Value obj.
correspondence.leanType: def renormalizedValue {Obj : Type u} {R : Type v} [Zero R] (CF : CutoffFunctional Obj R) (obj : Obj) : R

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Top-level logarithmic-anomaly accessor for a cutoff functional. In explicit terms, for obj a type, r a type, cf of type cutoff functional obj r, and obj of type obj, its defining expression is CF.anomaly Value obj.
correspondence.leanType: def anomalyValue {Obj : Type u} {R : Type v} [Zero R] (CF : CutoffFunctional Obj R) (obj : Obj) : R

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A packaged class of geometric objects carrying one common cutoff functional. This is the extension point shared by volume, curvature integrals, and future renormalized-area constructions. Its mathematical data are: object Type: Type u; cutoff Functional: Cutoff Functional object Type R.

references.locator: Proposition 3.7, Proposition 3.10, Lemma 4.1, and Theorems 1.1/1.4 as applicable
references.note: Primary source for the renormalized-curvature-integral mechanism represented by these modules.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
